sections.title: Starting the Quest
Trying to board the Adventurous before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think that whoever owns this ship will be happy with me wandering all over it.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Mumblelandlubbermumblemumble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: What right have ye to be so happy, landlubber?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it is quite a nice day.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye, that it is.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Will ye forgive an old sailor his temper [lad/lass/kid]? I'm in dire straits indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem? I may be able to help.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I dunno, [lad/lass/kid], I need a pirate to help me with my problem, but none will sail with me.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye look like ye might have the right stuff, tell me would ye like te be a pirate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've always wanted to be a pirate!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: It takes more grit than most have te be a pirate, and I don't think ye've got what it takes [lad/lass/kid].

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well [lad/lass/kid], I don't think ye could pirate yer way out of a chest full of piracy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awww...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: But since nobody else will risk the trip ye'll do!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well [lad/lass/kid], I be Bill Teach, the Captain of the Adventurous, one of the finest ships ever to sail these waters.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I'm in port doin' some legitimate tradin' before headin' back to Mos Le'Harmless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mos Le'Harmless? Where is that?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: It's a pirate stronghold off the southern coast of Morytania. Been in existence for years it has and I reckon it's the best place for pirates to relax!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, one of the best... A lot be liking the Pandemonium these days, and Brimhaven does have that fancy Agility Arena...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will it be OK for me to go there if I am not a pirate?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Of course [lad/lass/kid], once we make landfall ye'll have worked as a sailor on a pirate vessel, which is one of the easiest ways to become a true pirate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Worked as a sailor? Do I really need to do that? I've already got some sailing experience.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Of course ye do, [lad/lass/kid]! Plenty be claiming they've got what it takes to be a pirate, but I can't be takin' ye word 'till I see it for myself.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: This brings me back to the reason I'm stuck here of course, and the reason I need your help.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Last night I got into a war o' words with another Cap'n, and he has vowed to sink my ship and put all of my crew to the sword.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How horrible! How did your crew react?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: By desertin'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All of them?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Every last one!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: The second I leave port I'll come under attack. I think I'll be able te make it if I have another person helpin' out, but I wouldn't stand a chance by me self.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye said ye would help me out [lad/lass/kid], will ye stand by yer word?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I am a [man/woman] of my word.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Arr! Ye struck me as a [man/woman] of integrity!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: My ship is the Adventurous, and she's at the east dock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't...err...I mean ye'll not regret it Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I'm sure I won't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, if it's so dangerous your crew left, count me out.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, that doesn't surprise me much.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: On yer way [lad/lass/kid], leave me be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, it sounds quite dangerous.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye, that it be. Come back when yer liver ain't so lily-like, [lad/lass/kid].

sections.title: Speaking to Bill Teach again at the pub

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Let me finish my drink, landlubber!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If ye ain't up te scratch as a pirate I may not get another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye aye Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Just remember, [he/she]'s all ye could get, Bill, and maybe ye'll get through it...

sections.title: Speaking to Bill Teach on the ship
Talking to Bill Teach again before setting sail

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye all ready to go lad?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye came!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem rather surprised.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, I had the feelin' ye might have run off when ye realised how much of a task ye had ahead of ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't ye worry about me, Cap'n! I'm the scurviest, throat-cuttinest pirate to ever walk the mainplank! Arr!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye, I should have remembered ye was eager.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye all ready to go [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I just go grab something?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass], take yer time, I'm not goin' anywhere without ye.

sections.title: Cutscene on the ship
Teleporting out of the ship battle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it looks like they've found us Cap'n.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye don't say [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, without anyone else te help ye'll have te do some hard work if we don't want to end up walkin' home on the sea bed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you be doing?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well if their first shot hadn't taken out the cannon I'd be firing that at them.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Arr! They seek te hole the ship! Go and take care of their cannon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If ye grab some fuse from the gun locker, and a rope from the repair locker, then ye can use the rope on the sails to swing over and deal with their cannon.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Just take the fuse, use it on the barrel of gunpowder next to the cannon, and then light it with something.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: A lantern, a tinderbox, it doesn't matter.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: End of problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get back?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: They have masts as well don't they?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Just do the same trick using some rope on their sails and swing over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll hop to it Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Stop doin' that!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye ain't a pirate yet by a long shot!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I'll be on deck if ye need me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now to take care of that cannon!

sections.title: Dealing with the cannon
Trying to fill the ship hull before taking out the cannon
Talking to Bill Teach on the deck

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just to clear everything up, what is it you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye'll need to silence their cannon before it sinks us. Before ye do that though, ye'll need to swing over to their ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make a rope swing?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Yer not much of a pirate are ye lad?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye climb up the mast and use a rope on the sails up there.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye climb down and ye swing on the rope.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If I have te explain how ye do THAT then I might as well just send out some biscuit weevles to do the job instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye aye Cap'n! One rope swing coming right up!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Egad...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I blow up their cannon?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye'll need some fuse from the cannon locker below decks.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Swing over to the enemy ship and put the fuse in the barrel of gunpowder they'll have next to the cannon to speed up their firin'.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then, use something on the fuse te get it lit. A tinderbox, candle or any sort of flame will do.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: After that ye just step back and let it burn by its self.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll blow that cannon sky high, sez I!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Give me strength...

sections.title: Choosing the Return-Home option

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, Cap'n, can we go back to port?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Go back?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Got a little scared now have we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not that, I just need to grab something from the bank, that's all...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: A likely story.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Are ye sure ye can't get by without it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need to go back now.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I thought as much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: That's the spirit!

sections.title: Talking to Bill Teach in the pub after running away

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, as much as this is an interestin' break, why don't ye head back to the ship and we'll see if we can beat them this time?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Stop sayin' that!

sections.title: Talking to Bill Teach on the Adventurous after running away

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: All right, lad let's try this again. This time, try not te run away!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye all ready to go lad?

sections.title: The enemy reappears

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: One look at the enemy and ye run away.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Get over there and take out their cannon before I throw ye there me self.

sections.title: Talking to enemy pirates
Pirate 1

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Kill the squid-lovin' dog!

sections.title: Pirate 2

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I'll have ye guts fer garters!

sections.title: Pirate 3

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Kill the landlubber!

sections.title: Pirate 4

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Get off my ship!

sections.title: Pirate 5

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Te Davy-Jones locker with ye!

sections.title: Pirate 6

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Fancy a crumpet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Sorry, I mean...

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr! Run him through sez I!

sections.title: Pirate 7

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I'm gonna kill ye!

sections.title: Pirate 8

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Prepare te die!

sections.title: Trying to plunder the chest or ransack the barrel before dealing with the cannon
Using a rope swing
Falling on a rope swing
Using fuse on the barrel
Attempting to light the barrel instead of the fuse
Failing to light the fuse
Attempting to light the fuse using a Bullseye lantern
The fuse is lit!
Sort out the leaks
Talking to Bill Teach again before patching the leaks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr...I may well have been caught up in the whole 'pirate' thing, and I can't seem to remember what I need to do now...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Zounds...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Pay attention!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll need to get some planks from the repair locker and tack them in place. Then ye smear swamp paste over the planks to seal the hole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I be rememberin' now Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: This is what I get when I ask random strangers for help...

sections.title: Asking to return to port
Returning to battle after dealing with the cannon
Trying to patch a hole without planks
Trying to patch a hole without tacks
Trying to repair a hole without a hammer

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Looks like they haven't been back to port to get that cannon fixed, which is lucky.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Get below and fix the holes in the hull pronto, or we'll sink before they have a chance te kill us!

sections.title: Planking up a hole
Trying to waterproof a hole without swamp paste
Patching up a hole
After all the holes have been fixed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The holes be plugged Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Nailed shut and waterproofed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Good work [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Why not pay them a visit and go plunder their hold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll plunder their booty and no mistake!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Gadzooks...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Come back here and drop it in the chest in the hold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye aye Cap'n! One plunderin' comin' right up!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I've been a pirate all me life and I've never heard anyone keep harpin' on like that!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I've blown their cannon to smithereens Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Yer not a pirate yet [lad/lass], so stop talkin' like that!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye've sorted out their cannon, but we're takin' on water.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Go below deck and sort out the leaks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will smearing swamp paste on the holes do?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: No [lad/lass], these holes were made by cannon fire.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll need to get some planks from the repair locker and tack them in place. Then ye smear swamp paste over the planks to seal the hole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll have this ship ship-shape in no time! Arr!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Wait 'till we make landfall before ye start actin' like a pirate!

sections.title: Time to plunder
Talking to Bill Teach before plundering

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...how many loads of plunder did you want me to get?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ten! Can't ye count that high?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I can!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens if I take all the plunder I find and there is less than ten loads?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye wait for the pirates to stash some more in the hold. Since they are under attack they'll be dumpin' their gold and stuff in there every chance they get.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Just give it a minute and head back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll keep plunderin' till the hold be full of booty!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Yer not making any friends here [lad/lass], just go and do it.

sections.title: Asking to return to port
Returning to plunder from the port

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: When yer quite done runnin' away lad ye've got a load of plunder to be grabbin'!

sections.title: Plundering the chest
Looting the crate
Ransacking the barrel
Storing the loot in the storage
Filling up the storage
Counting the plunder in the storage
Attempting to plunder more after filling up the storage
Fixing up the cannon
Talking to Bill Teach before fixing the cannon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If ye know what is good for ye, yer off to fix the cannon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How was I going to do that again?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: By replacing the broken barrel with a new one! Hop to it [lad/lass], the salt air is clearly eatin' into yer brains!

sections.title: Returning to fix the cannon from the port

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Have ye sorted out the cannon yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then hop to it lad!

sections.title: Taking a new barrel from the gun locker
Attempting to take other items from the gun locker
Using a new barrel on the cannon
Talking to Bill Teach after replacing the barrel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cap'n I...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Have ye fixed the cannon yet, yes or no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye did? Great!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Now ye can give those pirates a good shot across their bows.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll need to load the cannon, and if ye get it wrong the barrel will blow, and ye'll need to start again.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: First, ye take some powder from the powder keg.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye use a ramrod from the gun locker on the powder to tamp the charge.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye use a canister round on the cannon.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Finally ye use some fuse on the cannon, and light it like ye did the fuse on the barrel earlier.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then, ye'll need to use the ramrod on the cannon to clean it out for the next shot.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye got all that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but if I forget I'll come and ask you.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: And te think this was all I could get te help...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I warn ye, [lad/lass], if the first word out of yer mouth is 'Arr' I'll be wearing yer spleen as a novelty hat before ye reach the second 'r'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahoy shipmate!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I should have seen that comin'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cap'n! I've put ten loads of plunder in the chest, in the hold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I even had some left over!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Let me see that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have those back?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Have what back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are we all done now?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Not yet! Let's give em a blast with the cannon before we leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How, the cannon is broken.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Alright [lad/lass], try and stay with me on this one.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: See, there is a secret technique for making things that be broken right again.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: We call this magical process...repairin'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what repairing is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair the cannon?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye need to remove the old barrel, then replace it with a new one from the gun locker downstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I don't want to overload ye with too many details right now. Come tell me when the cannon is ready again.

sections.title: Talking to Bill Teach again before firing the cannon
Returning to fire the cannon from the port

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: We were on the home stretch lad why did we turn back?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Never mind, just go shoot the pirates and let's press on.

sections.title: Using the cannon in the correct order
Taking powder from the barrel
Blowing up the cannon
Inspecting the cannon
Attempting to load a steel cannonball into the cannon
Cleaning the cannon
Firing the cannon
Firing the cannon without adding powder or ammunition
Firing the cannon without adding ammunition

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I can guess from the lack of an explosion that ye haven't fired the cannon yet, correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, not as such no.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: And I'll bet ye want a quick recap of how to do it, correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would help to be honest.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll need to load the cannon, and if ye get it wrong the barrel will blow, and ye'll need to start again.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: First, ye take some powder from the powder keg.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye use a ramrod from the gun locker on the powder to tamp the charge.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then ye use a canister round on the cannon.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Finally ye use some fuse on the cannon, and light it like ye did the fuse on the barrel earlier.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then, ye'll need to use the ramrod on the cannon to clean it out for the next shot.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye got all that this time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Yer still not a pirate, so cut that out!

sections.title: Sinking the enemy ship
Talking to Bill Teach before sinking the enemy ship

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cap'n, I know I need to load and fire the cannon at the enemy ship...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: and I know I do this by adding powder to the cannon, tamping the charge with a ramrod, adding a cannonball, adding some fuse and then lighting it...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I also remembered your warning about cleaning the barrel after every shot to avoid the cannon exploding...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've forgotten how many times I'm supposed to shoot the enemy ship...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Three! Three times [lad/lass]! Three! One more than two, one less than four! Got it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is no need to shout...

sections.title: Returning to sinking from the port

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Were almost there! Just hole their hull and we'll be able to make it past. Get to it!

sections.title: Fire at will!
Shooting at the hull again
After the third and final hit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that seems to have showed them Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Great! This ordeal will soon be over!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye Cap'n, we'll beat these pirates yet!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Pirates?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Yes, that ordeal too!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Now [lad/lass], we'll send them to the bottom of the sea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye'll shoot holes in their hull with the cannon.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Essentially ye do what ye just did, but use a cannonball from the gun locker in the hold instead of the canister rounds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! I'll sink them scurvy dogs!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Again with the 'Arr!'...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Give it three tries and see if they sink, that many holes should do it.

sections.title: End of quest cutscene
Claiming the loot from Bill Teach after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have that gold now?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Sure ye can. Here.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye've done it [lad/lass]! They're sunk now!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Let's head to the island!

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.text: Bill? You look like you've been to Davey Jones' locker and back.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Oh, Miss La'Fiette, if only I were that lucky.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: My crew deserted and I was stuck with an adventurer mannin' me ship.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: [He/She] was as piratical as a bunch of Dwellberries...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am still here you know...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass], I remember.

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.text: I'll leave you two alone.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: All right...

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ok [player name], yer now a pirate, but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: BUT ye can't just go around talking like some pirate in a fancy play.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: No self-respectin' pirate says 'Arr' that much!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, except for Fancy Dan, but that be another story.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Here, have this book. It'll learn ye how to be a pirate.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Or at least learn ye te talk proper like one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So all I have to do is read this book and I'm a pirate?

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye...that be all ye need te do.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll need that book if ye want te trade with some of the pirates round here.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Outsiders tend not te understand them if ye catch me drift.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Anyway, I'd have been shark bait without ye.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If ye get a moment come see me fer yer share of the loot.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I'm sure as soon as ye stop yellin' 'Arr!' ye'll make a great pirate.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Thanks [player name]. If ye need a lift back, just come see me.
